_comment: Exercises. The first block is taken word-for-word from the hospital physio booklet pages that were readable. The 'standard_set' block holds the usual remaining total-knee-replacement exercises as editable placeholders – CONFIRM these against the booklet/physiotherapist and edit reps. General guidance from the booklet: exercise 20–30 minutes, 2–3 times a day during early recovery; do exercises lying on your back with legs slightly apart.

exercises.name: Deep breaths
exercises.detail: Take 5 deep breaths to keep the lungs clear (especially hourly when resting in bed).

exercises.name: 1. Ankle pumps
exercises.detail: Move your feet up and down rhythmically by tightening the calf and shin muscles. Helps prevent blood clots.

exercises.name: 2. Ankle rotations
exercises.detail: Move your ankles in circles, clockwise and anticlockwise.

exercises.name: 3. Quadriceps sets (static quads)
exercises.detail: Lying down, tighten the thigh muscle and press the back of the knee down into the bed. Hold, then relax.

exercises.name: 4. Gluteal sets
exercises.detail: Squeeze your buttock muscles together, hold, then relax.

exercises.name: 5. Heel slides
exercises.detail: Slide the heel of the operated leg up towards the buttock to bend the knee, then slide back down.

exercises.name: 6. Straight leg raises
exercises.detail: With the knee straight, lift the whole leg a few inches off the bed, hold, then lower slowly.

exercises.name: 7. Short-arc quads
exercises.detail: Place a rolled towel under the knee; straighten the knee by lifting the foot, keeping the knee on the towel. Hold, then lower.

exercises.name: 8. Knee extension stretch
exercises.detail: Sit with the heel propped on a stool and the knee unsupported; let the knee straighten under its own weight.

exercises.name: 9. Seated knee bends
exercises.detail: Sitting on a firm chair, slide the foot back to bend the knee as far as comfortable, then straighten.
